Https.// Enabled: Serve over TLS. On by default. Kestrel binds HttpPort (HTTP, kept on so existing clients keep working) and HttpsPort (TLS). A self-signed cert is auto-generated for this host and stored in the machine cert store — distribute its public cert to clients' Trusted Root, or set PfxPath/PfxPassword to use your own. A cert failure degrades to HTTP-only rather than failing startup. Set HttpPort to 0 to drop HTTP once all clients are on HTTPS.

Client.// InstallerPath: Absolute or content-root-relative path to the tray installer the /download/client endpoint serves. Blank uses 'client/OpenPrintDeploy.Client.Installer.exe' next to the server exe (where Publish-Server.ps1 puts it).

Updates.// GitHubRepo: owner/repo whose latest GitHub release the admin 'Check for updates' button compares against. Blank disables the check.

Auth.// AdminScheme: How the admin browser UI signs in (production only). 'Basic' = prompt for a domain username/password validated against AD (default). 'Negotiate' = legacy Windows SSO. Set to 'Negotiate' and restart to revert without reinstalling.

Admin.// Groups/Users: Break-glass admin grants honoured IN ADDITION to the Settings page, by domain name. Edit + restart to recover access if the Settings page locks you out. When Groups, Users, GroupSids and the Settings-page list are all empty, any authenticated user is an admin.
